text: Context Engineering is deciding what information reaches the LLM.
Token budgets are limited.
Retrieval returns candidate chunks.
Reranking improves the quality of selected context.
Long contexts can degrade model performance.
Irrelevant chunks should be filtered out before reaching the model.
Preserving document order can sometimes be more important than score.
Chunking strategy affects retrieval quality significantly.
Metadata filtering helps reduce noise in retrieved results.
Some high-scoring chunks may still be irrelevant to the query.
Context window size is a hard constraint in most LLMs.
Diversity in retrieved chunks can improve answer quality.
Prompt construction is the final step that turns selected context into a usable input for the LLM.
Budget-aware selection prevents context overflow and improves efficiency.
Lost in the middle phenomenon causes models to ignore information in long contexts.
Hybrid search combines semantic similarity with keyword matching for better recall.
Context compression techniques remove redundant information while preserving meaning.
Parent-child chunking preserves hierarchical relationships in documents.
Query rewriting can significantly improve retrieval effectiveness for complex questions.
Evaluation of RAG systems requires separate metrics for retrieval and generation quality.
System prompts define the overall behavior and constraints of the LLM.
Adaptive retrieval decides when and how much context is needed based on query complexity.
